normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem inorder_strictly_ascending_of_bst {t : Tree α} (hbst : BST t) (hin : InorderStrict t) : Inorder t = sort (Inorder t) ∧ InorderStrict t
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Assume the input tree is a BST and its in-order traversal is strictly ascending; this provides the ordering property needed for correctness.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem keys_present {t : Tree α} (hbst : BST t) {k1 k2 : α} (hk1 : Mem k1 t) (hk2 : Mem k2 t) : ∃ n : Node α, Mem k1 (subtree n) ∧ Mem k2 (subtree n)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Since both keys are present in the tree, there exists at least one node whose subtree contains both keys.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def lowest_both (t : Tree α) (k1 k2 : α) : Prop := ∃ n, Mem k1 (subtree n) ∧ Mem k2 (subtree n) ∧ ∀ m, (m ≠ n → ¬ (Mem k1 (subtree m) ∧ Mem k2 (subtree m))) ∧ (n is ancestor of all nodes with both keys)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the target property: the 'lowest' node is the one that contains both keys and is below (i.e., is not above) any other node that also contains both keys.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem lowest_both_unique {t : Tree α} (hbst : BST t) {k1 k2 : α} (hk1 : Mem k1 t) (hk2 : Mem k2 t) : ∃! n, Mem k1 (subtree n) ∧ Mem k2 (subtree n) ∧ LowestNode n t k1 k2
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Under BST + strict in-order (no duplicates), the lowest node containing both keys is unique.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: def answer_key (t : Tree α) (k1 k2 : α) : α := (lowest_node t k1 k2).key
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The desired output is the key stored at the lowest node whose subtree contains both keys.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem impl_returns_some_key {t : Tree α} (hbst : BST t) {k1 k2 : α} (hk1 : Mem k1 t) (hk2 : Mem k2 t) : ∃ r, rust_impl t k1 k2 = some r
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Show the reference implementation returns a key (not None / error) when both keys are present.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem impl_invariant_subtree_contains {t : Tree α} (hbst : BST t) {k1 k2 : α} (hk1 : Mem k1 t) (hk2 : Mem k2 t) : let r := rust_impl t k1 k2; ∀ step, current_node step has both keys in its subtree
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove the loop/recursion invariant: at each step, the current candidate node’s subtree still contains both keys.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem impl_progress_towards_lowest {t : Tree α} (hbst : BST t) {k1 k2 : α} (hk1 : Mem k1 t) (hk2 : Mem k2 t) : rust_impl t k1 k2 terminates ∧ candidate_node moves strictly downward when possible
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Prove termination and that the algorithm moves downward toward the lowest node that can still contain both keys.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem impl_result_contains_both {t : Tree α} (hbst : BST t) {k1 k2 : α} (hk1 : Mem k1 t) (hk2 : Mem k2 t) : let r := rust_impl t k1 k2; Mem k1 t_subtree_of_result r ∧ Mem k2 t_subtree_of_result r
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned key corresponds to a node whose subtree contains both keys.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem impl_result_is_lowest {t : Tree α} (hbst : BST t) {k1 k2 : α} (hk1 : Mem k1 t) (hk2 : Mem k2 t) : let r := rust_impl t k1 k2; LowestNode (node_with_key r) t k1 k2
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The returned node is the lowest node (no descendant below it contains both keys).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem impl_matches_spec {t : Tree α} (hbst : BST t) {k1 k2 : α} (hk1 : Mem k1 t) (hk2 : Mem k2 t) : rust_impl t k1 k2 = answer_key t k1 k2
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Overall correctness: the implementation’s returned key equals the key of the lowest node containing both keys.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement: theorem impl_respects_bst_ordering {t : Tree α} (hbst : BST t) {k1 k2 : α} : decisions_taken_by_impl_follow_inorder_order t k1 k2
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Justify that the branching decisions made by the algorithm are consistent with BST ordering (e.g., comparisons route the search correctly).
